title: Rule-Obj-When
description: JSON schema for creating or updating a Pega When rule (Rule-Obj-When). When rules evaluate boolean conditions using one or more condition clauses combined with AND/OR logic. Used by flows (decision shapes), validate rules, activities, and UI visibility conditions.

pyClassName.description: Target class the When rule applies to (e.g., 'MyOrg-MyApp-Work-MyCase').

pyRuleName.description: Rule name (PascalCase). Forms part of the rule's identity key.

pyBlockName.description: Internal block name; must match pyRuleName. Auto-derived from pyRuleName when omitted.

pyLabel.description: Human-readable display label shown in Dev Studio and rule references.

pyDescription.description: Developer-facing description of what the When rule evaluates.

pyRuleAvailable.description: Whether the rule is active. CRITICAL: must be 'No' during create (circular reference workaround), then set to 'Yes' via update-rule.

pyLogic.description: Boolean combinator referencing condition labels declared in pyCondition. Single condition: 'A'. Multiple: 'A AND B', 'A OR B', '(A AND B) OR C'. Must reference pyConditionLabel values, not property expressions.

pyDisplayPropertyLabels.description: When this checkbox is selected, the system substitutes the property's name (e.g., .pxCreateDateTime) with its descriptive label (e.g., "Create Date/Time").

pyCondition.description: Condition clauses — array of Embed-WhenConditions. Each entry defines one boolean condition.

pyPagesAndClasses.description: Named pages used by condition expressions.

pyParameters.description: Rule-level Parameters tab. Declares typed input parameters that callers pass into this When rule (e.g., `Param.threshold`, `Param.region`). Each entry is an `Embed-MethodParams` row with a name, a Pega data type (Text/Date/DateTime/Integer/Decimal/TimeofDay/TrueFalse/Double/Page), an optional description, default value, direction, and required flag. NOTE: this is NOT the same shape used inside `pyCondition[].pyFunctionData.pyParameters` — those are function-call slots (see `MethodParam`).

pyAllowedFunctions.description: Array of supported function aliases (Rule-Alias-Function) available for When rule conditions. Each entry defines a function's pyPurpose (used as pyConditionValue1Purpose), pyPatternText (used as pyEcho), and optional pyCity (FirstInList grouping key). Populated from pyFunctionAliasList.

pySkipBackwardChaining.description: Whether to skip backward chaining evaluation.

pyBaseRule.description: 'true' if this rule is the base rule of a circumstance set.

pyCircumstanceProp.description: Property used for circumstancing. Blank for no circumstancing.

pyValueChanged.description: Tracks whether the rule's value has changed since last save.

FunctionAliasName.description: Supported When-rule function alias names. Each value corresponds to a file in library-function-builder/examples/<alias>.md which defines its pyCallParams and pyFunctionData shape.

allOf.description: G2: When pyConditionValue1Purpose is non-empty, the condition must also supply pyFunctionData (full library template) and pyConditionValue1 (compiled expression). Sending only purpose+pyCallParams produces silently corrupt rules with empty pyFunctionData and the server returns 500 'Please specify a when condition' on pyConditionValue1.

pyConditionValue1.description: Comparison value or expression. String literals must be double-quoted within the string (e.g., '"High"'). Numeric values are unquoted (e.g., '80').

pyConditionValue1Purpose.description: Function alias (pyPurpose from Rule-Alias-Function) that determines how the condition is evaluated. Restricted to the When-rule supported subset of library-function-builder. Empty string allowed for placeholder/stub conditions. See library-function-builder/examples/ for each alias's pyCallParams and pyFunctionData shape.

pyConditionValue1String.description: Human-readable template (e.g., '[property reference] has a value'). Auto-derived by the server.

pyConditionValue1StringLabel.description: Populated template with actual values (e.g., '.Priority Equals "High"'). Auto-derived by the server.

pyCondValueCategory.description: Logical combinator with the previous condition: '' (none/first clause), 'AND', 'OR'.

pyClassName.description: Class context for this condition. Usually matches the rule's pyClassName.

pyFunctionData.description: Function metadata — Embed-UserFunction. Auto-populated based on pyConditionValue1Purpose.

pyCallParams.description: Named parameters for the function call (varies by pyConditionValue1Purpose).

pyTempText.description: Temporary text used during condition editing.

allOf.description: G3: When pyParameters has at least one entry, pyUIParameters must also have at least one entry. Empty pyUIParameters silently blanks pyConditionValue1String / pyConditionValue1StringLabel / pyFunctionData.pyLabel and renders the condition invisible in the form designer even though the underlying expression compiles correctly.

pyName.description: Function name matching pyConditionValue1Purpose. See library-function-builder/examples/<alias>.md for the corresponding pyFunctionData template.

pyCity.description: Grouping key. For CompareTwoValues this is 'FirstInList'. For other functions this often mirrors the function name (e.g., '1FreeFormExpressionBoolean', 'StringEqualsIgnoreCase'). Can also be empty.

pySignature.description: Function signature template (e.g., '@(Pega-RULES:ExpressionEvaluators).compareTwoValues({lValue}, "{comparator}", {rValue})'). Populated from the Rule-Alias-Function. Used as pySig in pyFunctionData.

pyEcho.description: Display echo text / pattern template (e.g., '[first value] [relation] [second value]'). Populated from the Rule-Alias-Function pyPatternText.

pyExpanded.description: Whether the function details are expanded in the UI.

pySmartPromptClass.description: Smart prompt class — used to scope property auto-complete to a specific class.

MethodParam.description: Function-call parameter slot (Context A). Used inside `pyCondition[].pyFunctionData.pyParameters`, `pyUIParameters`, and `pyCallParams` to wire a single argument slot of a function alias. `pyParametersParamType` here is a UI-rendering hint (Freeform / Values / HTMLProperty / Label / Alias / Textbox / Read / None), NOT a Pega data type. For the rule-level Parameters tab, use `RuleParameter` instead.

pyParametersParamType.description: UI-rendering hint for a function-call parameter slot (Context A: inside `pyCondition[].pyFunctionData.pyParameters` / `pyUIParameters` / `pyCallParams`). NOT a data type — see `RuleParameter.pyParametersParamType` for the rule-level Parameters-tab data-type enum. 'Freeform' allows free text entry. 'Values' restricts to a dropdown list. 'HTMLProperty' is used for expression builder inputs. 'Label' is a display-only label between parameters (static UI text in `pyUIParameters`). 'Alias' is used for date/time property inputs that resolve via alias. 'None' is used for hidden/auto-filled parameters. 'Textbox' is used for free-text inputs without property auto-complete. 'Read' is used for read-only parameters with pre-set values.

pyParametersParamDesc.description: Parameter description displayed as placeholder text (e.g., 'first value', 'relation', 'second value').

pyParametersParamDropdownValues.description: Controls the dropdown auto-complete type for this parameter. 'Property' enables property name auto-complete. 'OptionsList' uses pyParametersParamIntelliValidateAs as a pipe-delimited options list.

pyParametersParamIntelliValidateAs.description: Validation constraint for the parameter. Numeric values (e.g., '30') indicate max character length. Prefixed values (e.g., 'primary.pySymbolComparators') reference a data page or property for dropdown values list.

pyParametersDataType.description: Data type constraint for the parameter value. Typically empty for string parameters.

pyIsFunction.description: Whether this parameter is a function reference.

pyIsOptional.description: 'false' = parameter is required, 'true' = parameter is optional.

pyReference.description: Positional reference index (e.g., '1', '2', '3'). Used in pyUIParameters.

pyNodeCaption.description: Node caption for UI rendering. Typically matches the positional index.

RuleParameter.description: Rule-level Parameters tab entry (Context B). Used by the top-level `pyParameters` array on Rule-Obj-When to declare typed input parameters callers pass into the rule (referenced at runtime as `Param.<name>`). Each entry is an `Embed-MethodParams` row, but `pyParametersParamType` here is a Pega data type — NOT the UI-render hint used by `MethodParam`. Carries only the rule-parameter fields (name / type / description / default / direction / required) and does not use `pyParametersParamValue`, dropdown config, `pyReference`, or `pyNodeCaption`.

pyParametersParamType.description: Pega data type of the rule-level parameter. Stored in the Pretty form used by Rule-Obj-When / Rule-Declare-Expression instances (not the uppercase Rule-Obj-Activity form like STRING/INTEGER). 'Page' requires that the page's class be declared in `pyPagesAndClasses`.

pyParametersParamDesc.description: Human-readable description of the parameter, shown on the Parameters tab.

FunctionAlias.description: A supported function alias from the pyFunctionAliasList (Rule-Alias-Function). Defines a condition evaluation function available in When rules.

pxObjClass.description: 'Rule-Alias-Function' for function alias entries populated from pyFunctionAliasList; 'Embed-Restrictions-AllowedFunctions' for restriction entries that limit which functions are available.

pyPurpose.description: Function identifier — used as pyConditionValue1Purpose in condition clauses and pyName in pyFunctionData. Restricted to the When-rule supported subset. See library-function-builder/examples/.

pyPatternText.description: Human-readable pattern template displayed in the UI (e.g., '[first value] [relation] [second value]'). Used as pyEcho in pyFunctionData.

pyCity.description: Grouping key. Only 'FirstInList' for CompareTwoValues; other functions use their own name or empty.

pyFunctionName.description: Function name for Embed-Restrictions-AllowedFunctions entries. Used to specify which function is allowed/restricted.
